transcript: stuff um checking the battery was okay it was it's a new battery and if we just open this turn it over r18 is our starter motor relay which is here uh so we've removed this and if you look underneath using a little screwdriver if you use a little screwdriver on the underside you can flick those little clips off take the lid off the start of the relay and when you put it back in if you get an assistant to press your star button you should see the relay kicking you can test you've got a voltage on this side coming into the wheeler and you can prove that it switches over now the wire from here then goes down to the starter motor it goes no other places and when i tested that there was no voltage at the starter motor when trying to start it uh we tried shorting it out across the main live feed that comes from here through the fuse drops down goes round to the starter motor we tried shorting it between that and the engage and it did nothing when we tested it to earth we got readings like it should be starting but it did nothing we've had the computer plugged in found nothing so last night we discovered even though we've got our 12 volts from our main feed to our starter motor under load it loses its voltage so it comes down to a bad earth now we've checked here nothing and from here it comes across bolts to the car down this corner and all that is on and it's fine after a bit of research and i'll show you when i've stripped it down over the top of this wheel behind the liner is an earth strap that goes from the cab down to the chassis and then from the chassis it goes over to the engine so um we've proved it by putting a jump lead on the starter motor and then on the negative side of the starter motor and then onto the battery like that just running it around the car do you push to start the car starts first time so we know for a fact it's definitely enough issue so i'll show you the straps when i find them let's find out what's happened to them
